name: Bricks and Fired Masonry
tagline: Standardized fired units for durable construction and kilns
problem:
Unfired mud and irregular stone limit what you can build: walls erode in rain, joints shift, and repairs consume labor. Civilization needs repeatable structural units that can be produced in volume and assembled by many hands.

Fired bricks solve the repeatability problem. Standard sizes make arches, vaults, and kilns practical, and they enable durable urban infrastructure when timber or quality stone is scarce.
overview:
Brickmaking shapes clay-rich mixes into consistent blocks, dries them to remove water, then fires them to sinter minerals into a hard ceramic. The firing schedule matters: too cold and bricks remain weak; too hot and they vitrify or slump.

For recovery, bricks also bootstrap industry: better kilns enable better ceramics, lime, glass, and metal heat-treatment. Bricks are both a building material and a manufacturing enabler.

principles.name: Drying and shrinkage
principles.explanation: Water removal shrinks clay; controlled drying prevents cracking and warping.

principles.name: Sintering
principles.explanation: Firing bonds particles into a strong ceramic without fully melting the body.

principles.name: Thermal gradients
principles.explanation: Uneven heating creates stress; kiln design and stacking reduce gradients.

components.name: Clay body and temper
components.madeFrom: Clay plus sand/grog/straw depending on recipe

rawMaterials.name: Clay-rich soil
rawMaterials.spaceAlternatives: Regolith-based ceramics are plausible with binders and controlled sintering; early bricks may rely on sintered regolith blocks.
rawMaterials.processingRequired: Screen stones; adjust moisture; add temper to reduce cracking.

rawMaterials.name: Temper (sand, grog, ash, straw)
rawMaterials.purpose: Controls shrinkage and cracking; improves handling
earthLocations: Sand deposits; crushed fired ceramics; plant fibers
rawMaterials.spaceAlternatives: Screened regolith grains can serve as temper; keep dust under control.
rawMaterials.processingRequired: Sieve to consistent grain size; pre-dry organic tempers.

rawMaterials.name: Heat and kilns
rawMaterials.purpose: Calcination and firing for lime, bricks, and cement precursors
rawMaterials.spaceAlternatives: Electric kilns powered by solar/nuclear; thermal insulation is critical for energy efficiency.
rawMaterials.processingRequired: Temperature control, refractory linings, and safe ventilation.

buildSteps.title: Prepare clay body
buildSteps.description: Screen and knead clay; add temper until the mix forms without slumping or cracking on bending tests.
buildSteps.warningNote: Silica dust from dry materials is hazardous; dampen and ventilate.

buildSteps.title: Mold bricks
buildSteps.description: Use a consistent mold; pack firmly; strike off excess; demold onto drying boards.

buildSteps.title: Dry slowly
buildSteps.description: Dry under shade with airflow; flip bricks; avoid direct sun or wind that dries one face too fast.
buildSteps.warningNote: Fast drying cracks bricks; cracked bricks fail in walls.

buildSteps.title: Fire to target hardness
buildSteps.description: Stack to allow airflow; ramp temperature; hold; cool gradually. Keep a log for repeatability.
buildSteps.warningNote: Kilns are fire hazards; establish clear zones and extinguishing.

buildSteps.title: Sort and test
buildSteps.description: Reject underfired or cracked units; do simple ring and drop tests; keep best bricks for kilns and load-bearing work.
buildSteps.warningNote: Do not use weak bricks in structural arches or tall walls.

history.event: Fired bricks enable durable urban construction in multiple regions.
Standardized brick and tile systems scale construction.
Mechanized presses and continuous kilns increase consistency.

impact:
Bricks allow standardized construction, durable drainage, and scalable kilns. They also reduce reliance on large timber or high-quality stone.

In the tech tree, bricks are a platform technology for higher-temperature processes: lime, glass, and metallurgy improve when kilns become reliable.

sources.title: NPS Preservation Brief 2: Repointing Mortar Joints in Historic Masonry Buildings
sources.note: See Brief 2 for masonry mortar context and failure modes
